channel.title: Knews-Py Aggregated Feeds - All Aggregated News
channel.description: Aggregated feed for Knews-Py Aggregated Feeds - All Aggregated News

item.title: Some on-air claims about Dominion Voting Systems were false, Fox News acknowledges in statement after deal is announced
item.description: N/A
item.content:encoded: N/A

item.title: Dominion still has pending lawsuits against election deniers such as Rudy Giuliani and Sidney Powell
item.description: N/A
item.content:encoded: N/A

item.title: Here are the 20 specific Fox broadcasts and tweets Dominion says were defamatory
item.description: • Fox-Dominion trial delay 'is not unusual,' judge says • Fox News' defamation battle isn't stopping Trump's election lies
item.content:encoded: • Fox-Dominion trial delay 'is not unusual,' judge says • Fox News' defamation battle isn't stopping Trump's election lies

item.title: Judge in Fox News-Dominion defamation trial: 'The parties have resolved their case'
item.description: The judge just announced in court that a settlement has been reached in the historic defamation case between Fox News and Dominion Voting Systems.
item.content:encoded: The judge just announced in court that a settlement has been reached in the historic defamation case between Fox News and Dominion Voting Systems.

item.title: 'Difficult to say with a straight face': Tapper reacts to Fox News' statement on settlement
item.description: A settlement has been reached in Dominion Voting Systems' defamation case against Fox News, the judge for the case announced. The network will pay more than $787 million to Dominion, a lawyer for the ...
item.content:encoded: A settlement has been reached in Dominion Voting Systems' defamation case against Fox News, the judge for the case announced. The network will pay more than $787 million to Dominion, a lawyer for the company said.

item.title: Millions in the US could face massive consequences unless McCarthy can navigate out of a debt trap he set for Biden
item.description: • DeSantis goes to Washington, a place he once despised, looking for support to take on Trump • Opinion: For the GOP to win, it must ditch Trump • Chris Christie mulling 2024 White House bid • Analysi...
item.content:encoded: • DeSantis goes to Washington, a place he once despised, looking for support to take on Trump • Opinion: For the GOP to win, it must ditch Trump • Chris Christie mulling 2024 White House bid • Analysis: The fire next time has begun burning in Tennessee

item.title: White homeowner accused of shooting a Black teen who rang his doorbell turns himself in to face criminal charges
item.description: • 'A major part of Ralph died': Aunt of teen shot after ringing wrong doorbell speaks • 20-year-old woman shot after friend turned into the wrong driveway in upstate New York, officials say
item.content:encoded: • 'A major part of Ralph died': Aunt of teen shot after ringing wrong doorbell speaks • 20-year-old woman shot after friend turned into the wrong driveway in upstate New York, officials say

item.title: Newly released video shows scene of Jeremy Renner's snowplow accident
item.description: Newly released body camera footage shows firefighters and sheriff's deputies rushing to help actor Jeremy Renner after a near-fatal snowplow accident in January. The "Avengers" actor broke more than 3...
item.content:encoded: Newly released body camera footage shows firefighters and sheriff's deputies rushing to help actor Jeremy Renner after a near-fatal snowplow accident in January. The "Avengers" actor broke more than 30 bones and suffered other severe injuries. CNN's Chloe Melas has more.

item.title: Jake Gyllenhaal and Jamie Lee Curtis spent the Covid-19 lockdown together
item.description: It's sourdough bread and handstands for Jake Gyllenhaal and Jamie Lee Curtis.
item.content:encoded: It's sourdough bread and handstands for Jake Gyllenhaal and Jamie Lee Curtis.

item.title: Toddler crawls through White House fence, prompts Secret Service response
item.description: A tiny intruder infiltrated White House grounds Tuesday, prompting a swift response from the US Secret Service.
item.content:encoded: A tiny intruder infiltrated White House grounds Tuesday, prompting a swift response from the US Secret Service.

item.title: Jamie Foxx remains hospitalized nearly a week after 'medical complication'
item.description: Jamie Foxx remains hospitalized in Georgia nearly a week after his daughter revealed the actor experienced a "medical complication," a source with knowledge of the matter told CNN on Monday.
item.content:encoded: Jamie Foxx remains hospitalized in Georgia nearly a week after his daughter revealed the actor experienced a "medical complication," a source with knowledge of the matter told CNN on Monday.

item.title: A 13-year-old dies after participating in a Benadryl TikTok 'challenge'
item.description: A 13-year-old in Ohio has died after "he took a bunch of Benadryl," trying a dangerous TikTok challenge that's circulating online, according to a CNN affiliate and a GoFundMe account from his family.
item.content:encoded: A 13-year-old in Ohio has died after "he took a bunch of Benadryl," trying a dangerous TikTok challenge that's circulating online, according to a CNN affiliate and a GoFundMe account from his family.

item.title: See pizza delivery guy take out suspect fleeing police
item.description: Pizza guy delivers more than a pie, taking out a fleeing suspect. CNN's Jeanne Moos shows him putting his best foot forward.
item.content:encoded: Pizza guy delivers more than a pie, taking out a fleeing suspect. CNN's Jeanne Moos shows him putting his best foot forward.

item.title: Netflix is winding down its DVD business after 25 years
item.description: Netflix is officially winding down the business that helped make it a household name.
item.content:encoded: Netflix is officially winding down the business that helped make it a household name.

item.title: FTC chair Lina Khan warns AI could 'turbocharge' fraud and scams
item.description: Artificial intelligence tools such as ChatGPT could lead to a "turbocharging" of consumer harms including fraud and scams, and the US government has substantial authority to crack down on AI-driven co...
item.content:encoded: Artificial intelligence tools such as ChatGPT could lead to a "turbocharging" of consumer harms including fraud and scams, and the US government has substantial authority to crack down on AI-driven consumer harms under existing law, members of the Federal Trade Commission said Tuesday.

item.title: Eating too much of these foods is driving the rise in type 2 diabetes, study says
item.description: Gobbling up too many refined wheat and rice products, along with eating too few whole grains, is fueling the growth of new cases of type 2 diabetes worldwide, according to a new study that models data...
item.content:encoded: Gobbling up too many refined wheat and rice products, along with eating too few whole grains, is fueling the growth of new cases of type 2 diabetes worldwide, according to a new study that models data through 2018.

item.title: ADHD medication abuse in schools is a 'wake-up call'
item.description: At some middle and high schools in the United States, 1 in 4 teens report they've abused prescription stimulants for attention deficit hyperactivity disorder during the year prior, a new study found.
item.content:encoded: At some middle and high schools in the United States, 1 in 4 teens report they've abused prescription stimulants for attention deficit hyperactivity disorder during the year prior, a new study found.

item.title: Apple CEO was presented with an original Macintosh. See his reaction
item.description: CEO Tim Cook personally welcomed customers to the new Apple store in Mumbai as the tech company opens its first retail stores in India. CNN's Vedika Sud reports.
item.content:encoded: CEO Tim Cook personally welcomed customers to the new Apple store in Mumbai as the tech company opens its first retail stores in India. CNN's Vedika Sud reports.

item.title: Democrats bash Justice Clarence Thomas but their plan to investigate ethics allegations is unclear
item.description: Senate Democrats railed against Justice Clarence Thomas on Tuesday amid reports that the Supreme Court conservative failed to disclose luxury travel, gifts and a real estate transaction involving a GO...
item.content:encoded: Senate Democrats railed against Justice Clarence Thomas on Tuesday amid reports that the Supreme Court conservative failed to disclose luxury travel, gifts and a real estate transaction involving a GOP megadonor, but their plan to investigate the conservative jurist remains unclear.

item.title: Russia is 'going backwards' in equipment and deploying post WWII-era tanks, according to Western officials
item.description: • Jailed Wall Street Journal reporter Evan Gershkovich denied detention appeal in Moscow • Putin visits Russian troops at military headquarters in Kherson • Watch moment WSJ journalist appears in Russ...
item.content:encoded: • Jailed Wall Street Journal reporter Evan Gershkovich denied detention appeal in Moscow • Putin visits Russian troops at military headquarters in Kherson • Watch moment WSJ journalist appears in Russian court

item.title: Two Russians claiming to be former Wagner commanders admit killing children and civilians in Ukraine
item.description: Two Russian men who claim to be former Wagner Group commanders have told a human rights activist that they killed children and civilians during their time in Ukraine.
item.content:encoded: Two Russian men who claim to be former Wagner Group commanders have told a human rights activist that they killed children and civilians during their time in Ukraine.

item.title: 'My stomach is hurting from laughing': Hear panelist's reaction to DeSantis' threat to Disney
item.description: CNN panelists react to Florida Gov. Ron DeSantis floating the idea of building a competing theme park next to Disney World in Orlando.
item.content:encoded: CNN panelists react to Florida Gov. Ron DeSantis floating the idea of building a competing theme park next to Disney World in Orlando.

item.title: GOP prepared to block vote to replace Feinstein on Senate Judiciary
item.description: Senate Majority Leader Chuck Schumer said on Tuesday that he hopes to replace Democratic Sen. Dianne Feinstein on the Senate Judiciary Committee with Sen. Ben Cardin of Maryland and aims to set up a f...
item.content:encoded: Senate Majority Leader Chuck Schumer said on Tuesday that he hopes to replace Democratic Sen. Dianne Feinstein on the Senate Judiciary Committee with Sen. Ben Cardin of Maryland and aims to set up a floor vote on the issue this afternoon, which Republicans are expected to block.

item.title: Oklahoma governor calls on officials to resign over recording of racist and threatening remarks
item.description: N/A
item.content:encoded: N/A

item.title: McCarthy slams Biden in handling of US debt
item.description: House Speaker Kevin McCarthy traveled to Wall Street on Monday to deliver a fresh warning that the House GOP majority will refuse to lift a cap on government borrowing unless Biden agrees to spending ...
item.content:encoded: House Speaker Kevin McCarthy traveled to Wall Street on Monday to deliver a fresh warning that the House GOP majority will refuse to lift a cap on government borrowing unless Biden agrees to spending cuts that would effectively neutralize his domestic agenda.

item.title: US warns Russia not to touch American nuclear technology at Ukrainian nuclear plant
item.description: The US has sensitive nuclear technology at a nuclear power plant inside Ukraine and is warning Russia not to touch it, according to a letter the US Department of Energy sent to Russia's state-owned nu...
item.content:encoded: The US has sensitive nuclear technology at a nuclear power plant inside Ukraine and is warning Russia not to touch it, according to a letter the US Department of Energy sent to Russia's state-owned nuclear energy firm Rosatom last month.

item.title: Repeated gunshots fired on live TV as ex-lawmaker shot by assassins
item.description: Atiq Ahmed, a former lawmaker in India's parliament, convicted of kidnapping, was shot dead along with his brother while police were escorting them for a medical check-up in a slaying caught on live t...
item.content:encoded: Atiq Ahmed, a former lawmaker in India's parliament, convicted of kidnapping, was shot dead along with his brother while police were escorting them for a medical check-up in a slaying caught on live television on Saturday. CNN's Vedika Sud reports.

item.title: FDA clears the way for additional bivalent boosters for certain vulnerable individuals
item.description: The U.S. Food and Drug Administration amended the terms of its emergency use authorizations for the Pfizer and Moderna bivalent vaccines on Tuesday, allowing people ages 65 and older and certain peopl...
item.content:encoded: The U.S. Food and Drug Administration amended the terms of its emergency use authorizations for the Pfizer and Moderna bivalent vaccines on Tuesday, allowing people ages 65 and older and certain people with weakened immunity to get additional doses before this fall's vaccination campaigns.

item.title: Maine authorities detained a person of interest after 4 people were found dead in a home and 3 others shot while driving
item.description: Maine authorities have detained a person of interest and continue to investigate after two shooting incidents that appear to be connected left at least four people dead and three others injured, state...
item.content:encoded: Maine authorities have detained a person of interest and continue to investigate after two shooting incidents that appear to be connected left at least four people dead and three others injured, state police said.

item.title: Southwest says flights resumed after delays caused by 'tech issues'
item.description: • Delta Air Lines reports record bookings for summer travel • Air France and Airbus acquitted in trial over 2009 plane crash
item.content:encoded: • Delta Air Lines reports record bookings for summer travel • Air France and Airbus acquitted in trial over 2009 plane crash

item.title: Damar Hamlin cleared to resume football activities after January cardiac arrest
item.description: Buffalo Bills safety Damar Hamlin, who has been cleared to resume football activities, said Tuesday his cardiac arrest during an NFL game in January was caused by commotio cordis.
item.content:encoded: Buffalo Bills safety Damar Hamlin, who has been cleared to resume football activities, said Tuesday his cardiac arrest during an NFL game in January was caused by commotio cordis.

item.title: Pilot makes history after landing on top of a 56-story hotel
item.description: Polish pilot Lukasz Czepiela made history after landing a plane on a helipad at the top of a 56-story hotel in Dubai.
item.content:encoded: Polish pilot Lukasz Czepiela made history after landing a plane on a helipad at the top of a 56-story hotel in Dubai.

item.title: Top US Navy admiral defends non-binary sailor amid some Republican criticism
item.description: The top US Navy admiral ardently defended a non-binary sailor on Tuesday amid some criticism from Republican lawmakers, saying he is "particularly proud of this sailor."
item.content:encoded: The top US Navy admiral ardently defended a non-binary sailor on Tuesday amid some criticism from Republican lawmakers, saying he is "particularly proud of this sailor."

item.title: Fulton County DA says fake Trump electors are incriminating one another and wants lawyer disqualified
item.description: The Fulton County District Attorney's office said some fake electors for Donald Trump have implicated each other in potential criminal activity and is seeking to disqualify their lawyer, according to ...
item.content:encoded: The Fulton County District Attorney's office said some fake electors for Donald Trump have implicated each other in potential criminal activity and is seeking to disqualify their lawyer, according to a new court filing.

item.title: High speed trains are racing across the world. But not in America
item.description: High speed trains have proved their worth across the world over the past 50 years.
item.content:encoded: High speed trains have proved their worth across the world over the past 50 years.

item.title: Podcast: One country musician is calling for other artists to oppose assault rifles
item.description: N/A
item.content:encoded: N/A

item.title: Here's what you need to know if you haven't filed your return yet — and even if you have
item.description: It's April 18, the official deadline to file your federal and state income tax returns for 2022. (It is also, apparently, National Animal Crackers Day for those who celebrate.)
item.content:encoded: It's April 18, the official deadline to file your federal and state income tax returns for 2022. (It is also, apparently, National Animal Crackers Day for those who celebrate.)

item.title: Undocumented immigrants are paying their taxes today, too
item.description: It's a surprising fact that's often overlooked in the immigration debate.
item.content:encoded: It's a surprising fact that's often overlooked in the immigration debate.

item.title: Opinion: Why millionaires like us want to pay more in taxes
item.description: Tuesday is Tax Day in America, one of the most stressful days of the year, when many taxpayers will finally end their procrastination, file their federal returns, and hope for a refund from the IRS. B...
item.content:encoded: Tuesday is Tax Day in America, one of the most stressful days of the year, when many taxpayers will finally end their procrastination, file their federal returns, and hope for a refund from the IRS. But for many of the nation's wealthiest, it's just another Tuesday.

item.title: 'World's longest' purpose-built cycling tunnel opens in Norway
item.description: There are many ways to explore the seven mountains that surround the picturesque UNESCO World Heritage city of Bergen on Norway's fjord-studded west coast. The newest, however, might well be record-br...
item.content:encoded: There are many ways to explore the seven mountains that surround the picturesque UNESCO World Heritage city of Bergen on Norway's fjord-studded west coast. The newest, however, might well be record-breaking.

item.title: Artist rejects photo prize after AI-generated image wins award
item.description: A German artist has rejected an award from a prestigious international photography competition after revealing that his submission was generated by Artificial Intelligence (AI).
item.content:encoded: A German artist has rejected an award from a prestigious international photography competition after revealing that his submission was generated by Artificial Intelligence (AI).

item.title: These ships disappeared in Lake Superior a century ago. Watch as they're found again
item.description: The Great Lakes Shipwreck Historical Society has found two of three ships that sank in the same Lake Superior storm more than a century ago, locating one in 2021 and the other in 2022.
item.content:encoded: The Great Lakes Shipwreck Historical Society has found two of three ships that sank in the same Lake Superior storm more than a century ago, locating one in 2021 and the other in 2022.

item.title: Erotic images of seniors show sex and intimacy in new light
item.description: What does intimacy look like for seniors? There's no end to sex scenes and other steamy content featuring the young and unwrinkled, but past a certain age, popular culture largely draws a blank — or t...
item.content:encoded: What does intimacy look like for seniors? There's no end to sex scenes and other steamy content featuring the young and unwrinkled, but past a certain age, popular culture largely draws a blank — or treats sex as a punchline.

item.title: China's economy is off to a solid start, rising 4.5% in Q1 2023
item.description: China's economy is off to a solid start in 2023 following its emergence from three years of strict pandemic restrictions.
item.content:encoded: China's economy is off to a solid start in 2023 following its emergence from three years of strict pandemic restrictions.

item.title: Even when wives make as much as husbands, they still do more at home
item.description: • Four out of the five US metro areas with the lowest unemployment are in Florida. Here's why • Opinion: The overlooked problem with raising the retirement age for Social Security
item.content:encoded: • Four out of the five US metro areas with the lowest unemployment are in Florida. Here's why • Opinion: The overlooked problem with raising the retirement age for Social Security

item.title: McDonald's is upgrading its burgers
item.description: McDonald's, which has been focusing on upgrading its core items to boost sales, is rolling out a series of changes designed to improve its signature burgers.
item.content:encoded: McDonald's, which has been focusing on upgrading its core items to boost sales, is rolling out a series of changes designed to improve its signature burgers.

item.title: Google-parent stock drops on fears it could lose search market share to AI-powered rivals
item.description: Shares of Google-parent Alphabet fell more than 3% in early trading Monday after a report sparked concerns that its core search engine could lose market share to AI-powered rivals, including Microsoft...
item.content:encoded: Shares of Google-parent Alphabet fell more than 3% in early trading Monday after a report sparked concerns that its core search engine could lose market share to AI-powered rivals, including Microsoft's Bing.

item.title: Mother's Day is around the corner. Here are 50+ thoughtful gifts she'll love
item.description: N/A
item.content:encoded: N/A

item.title: Why Trump’s constant attacks and his sniping with Elon Musk helps him and keeps the media scrambling
item.description: From Elon Musk, to Terry Moran, to the Los Angeles riots, Donald Trump is a president who relishes the news cycle and loves being at war.
item.content:encoded: From Elon Musk, to Terry Moran, to the Los Angeles riots, Donald Trump is a president who relishes the news cycle and loves being at war.

item.title: Diddy's ex's testimony could provide powerful corroboration to allegations in sex crimes trial: expert
item.description: Legal experts evaluate testimony from Diddy's ex, who claimed coercion and ongoing financial support, potentially corroborating allegations in sex crimes trial.
item.content:encoded: Legal experts evaluate testimony from Diddy's ex, who claimed coercion and ongoing financial support, potentially corroborating allegations in sex crimes trial.

item.title: Panthers and Oilers engage in all-out brawl as Florida takes control of Stanley Cup Final in Game 3 victory
item.description: Panthers take commanding 2-1 Stanley Cup Final lead with 6-1 victory over Oilers in Game 3 marked by a third-period brawl, multiple ejections, and historic scoring from Brad Marchand.
item.content:encoded: Panthers take commanding 2-1 Stanley Cup Final lead with 6-1 victory over Oilers in Game 3 marked by a third-period brawl, multiple ejections, and historic scoring from Brad Marchand.

item.title: Defense attorney's dramatic courtroom move has legal experts talking in Karen Read murder trial
item.description: Special prosecutor Hank Brennan admits evidence mistake in Karen Read's murder retrial as defense requests mistrial, with legal experts divided on whether the error was intentional
item.content:encoded: Special prosecutor Hank Brennan admits evidence mistake in Karen Read's murder retrial as defense requests mistrial, with legal experts divided on whether the error was intentional

item.title: DOJ identifies suspect accused of assaulting federal officer, as Trump vows tough response
item.description: Attorney General Bondi announces FBI has identified suspect who allegedly injured a federal officer by throwing rocks at vehicles during anti-ICE demonstrations.
item.content:encoded: Attorney General Bondi announces FBI has identified suspect who allegedly injured a federal officer by throwing rocks at vehicles during anti-ICE demonstrations.

item.title: Florida A&M athletic director turns herself in to police over $24K theft allegations from previous job
item.description: Angela Suggs, Florida A&M's athletic director, faces felony theft charges after allegedly stealing funds from the Florida Sports Foundation through falsified vouchers.
item.content:encoded: Angela Suggs, Florida A&M's athletic director, faces felony theft charges after allegedly stealing funds from the Florida Sports Foundation through falsified vouchers.

item.title: Mayor Bass blames Trump's ICE raids for starting riots while claiming 'things in LA are calm'
item.description: Los Angeles Mayor Karen Bass criticized the Trump administration's immigration raids, calling them "a recipe for pandemonium" that provoked protests.
item.content:encoded: Los Angeles Mayor Karen Bass criticized the Trump administration's immigration raids, calling them "a recipe for pandemonium" that provoked protests.

item.title: Louisiana authorities arrest girlfriend of escaped inmate for conspiracy in coordinated jailbreak operation
item.description: The Louisiana Attorney General announced former Orleans Parish Sheriff's employee Darriana Burton has been arrested for allegedly helping boyfriend Derrick Groves escape from jail.
item.content:encoded: The Louisiana Attorney General announced former Orleans Parish Sheriff's employee Darriana Burton has been arrested for allegedly helping boyfriend Derrick Groves escape from jail.

item.title: Christian Slater opens up about 'weird' reality of fame after 5 decades in Hollywood
item.description: Christian Slater told Fox News Digital "fame is always weird" after accepting his star on the Hollywood Walk of Fame, an achievement he called "a dream come true."
item.content:encoded: Christian Slater told Fox News Digital "fame is always weird" after accepting his star on the Hollywood Walk of Fame, an achievement he called "a dream come true."

item.title: Hailey Bieber's bikini photos prompt celebrities to flood comments with praise
item.description: Hailey Bieber's Instagram features bikini pictures and Rhode products as the founder enjoys summer while celebrating her brand's recent $1 billion acquisition.
item.content:encoded: Hailey Bieber's Instagram features bikini pictures and Rhode products as the founder enjoys summer while celebrating her brand's recent $1 billion acquisition.

item.title: California files lawsuit against DOJ over transgender athlete demand
item.description: California filed a lawsuit against the Justice Department over its demand that public school districts certify they will keep trans athletes out of girls' and women's sports.
item.content:encoded: California filed a lawsuit against the Justice Department over its demand that public school districts certify they will keep trans athletes out of girls' and women's sports.

item.title: Police swiftly remove anti-ICE protesters who invaded Trump Tower as nationwide demonstrations intensify
item.description: NYPD arrests 24 protesters occupying Trump Tower lobby during anti-ICE demonstration as similar protests emerge across the country in response to ICE operations in Los Angele
item.content:encoded: NYPD arrests 24 protesters occupying Trump Tower lobby during anti-ICE demonstration as similar protests emerge across the country in response to ICE operations in Los Angele

item.title: Democratic lawmaker looks forward to Elon Musk 'turning his fire against MAGA Republicans' in 2026 midterms
item.description: Rep. Ro Khanna, D-Calif., claimed that he has spoken with “senior confidants" of Tesla CEO Elon Musk about 2026 after his feud with President Donald Trump.
item.content:encoded: Rep. Ro Khanna, D-Calif., claimed that he has spoken with “senior confidants" of Tesla CEO Elon Musk about 2026 after his feud with President Donald Trump.

item.title: North Carolina great Rashad McCants dishes on the NBA Finals, Michael Jordan, Bill Belichick and more
item.description: UNC great Rashad McCants talked all about his thoughts on the NBA Finals, Bill Belichick heading to his alma mater, and Michael Jordan in a lengthy interview with OutKick.
item.content:encoded: UNC great Rashad McCants talked all about his thoughts on the NBA Finals, Bill Belichick heading to his alma mater, and Michael Jordan in a lengthy interview with OutKick.

item.title: Musk signals potential softening of feud with simple one emoji response to clip of Trump wishing him well
item.description: Signs continue to point to a softening in the relationship between President Trump and Elon Musk after Musk posted a heart emoji in response to Trump wishing him well.
item.content:encoded: Signs continue to point to a softening in the relationship between President Trump and Elon Musk after Musk posted a heart emoji in response to Trump wishing him well.

item.title: Power players or baseball players? The history behind the Congressional Baseball Game
item.description: The Congressional Baseball Game is an odd, but longstanding, tradition on the hill. Here's a look at what to expect this year, brought to you by yours truly.
item.content:encoded: The Congressional Baseball Game is an odd, but longstanding, tradition on the hill. Here's a look at what to expect this year, brought to you by yours truly.

item.title: RFK Jr scraps vaccine committee members in effort to restore 'public trust'
item.description: HHS removes Biden-appointed vaccine advisory committee members as department chief RFK Jr. pledges to prioritize "the restoration of public trust."
item.content:encoded: HHS removes Biden-appointed vaccine advisory committee members as department chief RFK Jr. pledges to prioritize "the restoration of public trust."

item.title: 113 House Dems vote against GOP resolution to condemn Boulder attack on pro-Israel activists
item.description: The House of Representatives voted on a pair of bills aimed at condemning anti-Semitism in the wake of the attack on pro-Israel demonstrators in Boulder, Colorado.
item.content:encoded: The House of Representatives voted on a pair of bills aimed at condemning anti-Semitism in the wake of the attack on pro-Israel demonstrators in Boulder, Colorado.

item.title: Charlamagne claims he hadn't heard about 'any ruckus or violence' in LA riot until National Guard arrived
item.description: Charlamagne tha God criticized the Trump administration's decision to deploy National Guard toops to Los Angeles County amid escalating anti-ICE protests in California.
item.content:encoded: Charlamagne tha God criticized the Trump administration's decision to deploy National Guard toops to Los Angeles County amid escalating anti-ICE protests in California.

item.title: 'Call Her Daddy' podcast host Alex Cooper accuses former college soccer coach of sexual harassment
item.description: "Call Her Daddy" podcast host Alex Cooper accused former Boston University women's soccer coach Nancy Feldman of sexual harassment in her docuemntary.
item.content:encoded: "Call Her Daddy" podcast host Alex Cooper accused former Boston University women's soccer coach Nancy Feldman of sexual harassment in her docuemntary.

item.title: Ex-NFL receiver Golden Tate details how Jalen Ramsey's treatment of his pregnant sister led to fight
item.description: Golden Tate discusses his feud with Dolphins cornerback Jalen Ramsey on "Bussin' with the Boys" podcast, explaining their infamous 2020 NFL confrontation.
item.content:encoded: Golden Tate discusses his feud with Dolphins cornerback Jalen Ramsey on "Bussin' with the Boys" podcast, explaining their infamous 2020 NFL confrontation.

item.title: Riley Gaines talks public's reaction to Simone Biles' personal attack: 'Almost feel bad for her'
item.description: Riley Gaine told Clay Travis on "Clay & Buck" that she almost felt bad for Simone Biles as social media reacted to the gymnast's personal attack on the former swimmer.
item.content:encoded: Riley Gaine told Clay Travis on "Clay & Buck" that she almost felt bad for Simone Biles as social media reacted to the gymnast's personal attack on the former swimmer.

item.title: Riley Gaines says Simone Biles 'sold out' young female athletes in heated debate over transgender sports
item.description: Former 12-time NCAA all-American swimmer Riley Gaines opens up about her 'X' feud with Simone Biles over transgender athletes in women's sports
item.content:encoded: Former 12-time NCAA all-American swimmer Riley Gaines opens up about her 'X' feud with Simone Biles over transgender athletes in women's sports

item.title: Chinese PhD student from Wuhan arrested smuggling biological materials after deleting electronic evidence: DOJ
item.description: Chinese researcher arrested for smuggling concealed biological materials from Wuhan to Michigan, as authorities find deleted electronic content and false customs statements.
item.content:encoded: Chinese researcher arrested for smuggling concealed biological materials from Wuhan to Michigan, as authorities find deleted electronic content and false customs statements.

item.title: Fox News Politics Newsletter: Arrest-ing Developments
item.description: Welcome to the Fox News Politics newsletter, with the latest updates on the Trump administration, Capitol Hill and more Fox News politics content.
item.content:encoded: Welcome to the Fox News Politics newsletter, with the latest updates on the Trump administration, Capitol Hill and more Fox News politics content.

item.title: Dozens of states sue to block the sale of 23andMe personal genetic data
item.description: States argue that biological samples, DNA data, health-related traits and medical records are too sensitive to be sold without each person's express, informed consent.
item.content:encoded: States argue that biological samples, DNA data, health-related traits and medical records are too sensitive to be sold without each person's express, informed consent.

item.title: Drug deaths plummet among young Americans as fentanyl carnage eases
item.description: U.S. drug deaths dropped by roughly 40% last year among people under the age of 35. It's a welcome pivot for families and communities devastated by fentanyl.
item.content:encoded: U.S. drug deaths dropped by roughly 40% last year among people under the age of 35. It's a welcome pivot for families and communities devastated by fentanyl.

item.title: What happened when Lyndon Johnson federalized the National Guard
item.description: President Lyndon B. Johnson federalized the National Guard in 1965, calling on troops to protect civil rights advocates who were marching from Selma, Ala., to Montgomery.
item.content:encoded: President Lyndon B. Johnson federalized the National Guard in 1965, calling on troops to protect civil rights advocates who were marching from Selma, Ala., to Montgomery.

item.title: Trump mobilizes Marines and more National Guard troops for duty in LA
item.description: U.S. Northern Command says the infantry battalion would be supporting the National Guard troops "who are protecting federal personnel and federal property in the greater Los Angeles area."
item.content:encoded: U.S. Northern Command says the infantry battalion would be supporting the National Guard troops "who are protecting federal personnel and federal property in the greater Los Angeles area."

item.title: RFK Jr. boots all members of the CDC's vaccine advisory committee
item.description: Health Secretary RFK Jr. has removed all 17 members of the CDC's Advisory Committee on Immunization Practices. He says replacing them with new members will help restore 'public trust' in vaccines.
item.content:encoded: Health Secretary RFK Jr. has removed all 17 members of the CDC's Advisory Committee on Immunization Practices. He says replacing them with new members will help restore 'public trust' in vaccines.

item.title: Students from India and China Rethink Studying in the U.S.
item.description: The United States hosts many students and scientific researchers from the world's two largest countries, India and China. The U.S. is seen as one of the best places to get and education and on the cut...
item.content:encoded: The United States hosts many students and scientific researchers from the world's two largest countries, India and China. The U.S. is seen as one of the best places to get and education and on the cutting edge of innovation. But a series of moves by the Trump administration, including greater scrutiny of those applying for student visa and, in the case of China, threats to revoke some visas, are making some rethink their plans.

item.title: Justin Baldoni's $400 million defamation suit against Blake Lively dismissed
item.description: Baldoni's $400 million lawsuit was in response to Lively accusing him of sexual harassment on the set of It Ends With Us.
item.content:encoded: Baldoni's $400 million lawsuit was in response to Lively accusing him of sexual harassment on the set of It Ends With Us.

item.title: Sly Stone, visionary funk frontman of the Family Stone, has died at age 82
item.description: The musical visionary led a multi-racial funk band that produced five Top 10 hits in the late 1960s and early '70s.
item.content:encoded: The musical visionary led a multi-racial funk band that produced five Top 10 hits in the late 1960s and early '70s.

item.title: Pam Bondi's brother overwhelmingly defeated in heated race to lead the D.C. Bar
item.description: The race became a microcosm for the clashes and pressures on the American legal system this year, in part because one of the two top candidates is the younger brother of Attorney General Pam Bondi.
item.content:encoded: The race became a microcosm for the clashes and pressures on the American legal system this year, in part because one of the two top candidates is the younger brother of Attorney General Pam Bondi.

item.title: Low turnout scuttles Italy referendum on citizenship
item.description: The referendum, which sought to reduce the time it takes to become a naturalized citizen from 10 years to five, failed because fewer than 50% of eligible voters took part in two days of voting.
item.content:encoded: The referendum, which sought to reduce the time it takes to become a naturalized citizen from 10 years to five, failed because fewer than 50% of eligible voters took part in two days of voting.
